problem: Six 6-sided dice are rolled.  What is the probability that the number of dice showing even numbers and the number of dice showing odd numbers is equal?

classified_questions.question: How would you define the event where the number of dice showing even numbers is equal to the number showing odd numbers?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why we choose to focus on 3 dice showing even numbers and 3 dice showing odd numbers? Is there any significance to this choice?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How many ways can we select 3 dice out of the 6 to show even numbers? Can you explain why this is represented by $\binom{6}{3}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Each roll of a single die has an equal probability of being even or odd. How does this information help us determine the probability of each arrangement of dice showing 3 odd and 3 even numbers?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Each arrangement of dice showing 3 odd and 3 even numbers occurs with a certain probability. What is that probability? Can you explain why it is equal to $\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{\!6}$?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Knowing the number of ways to have 3 dice showing even numbers and the probability of each arrangement, how can we determine the overall probability of the event we defined in question 1?
classified_questions.label: b
